verdict_reason: Low KD (20), decent volume (390), Reddit ranks #2 signaling content gap, small niche sites (picdefense.io, taxtosell.eu) rank well, clear tool intent with SaaS upgrade path
direction: Image Copyright Checker / Unauthorized Photo Use Finder

serp_analysis.opportunity_note: Reddit #2 and .edu site #3 prove gap for authoritative tool content. TinEye is reverse image search but not specifically a copyright checker. picdefense.io and taxtosell.eu are tiny new players ranking well. AI Overview present but tool intent means users need to actually use a tool, not just read about it.

trends.note: Too low for Google Trends detection but SEMrush shows consistent 390 monthly searches. Likely stable evergreen demand from photographers and content creators.

monetization.rationale: Small but targeted B2B audience (photographers, agencies, content creators). Free tier: check 5 images/month. Pro tier: unlimited checks, monitoring alerts, DMCA takedown templates. Affiliate links to Pixsy, CopyTrack, Getty contributor program.

execution_plan.mvp: Free image copyright checker tool: upload image or paste URL, tool checks Creative Commons metadata, reverse image searches, and provides copyright status assessment. DMCA takedown letter template generator.
execution_plan.content_strategy: Build content around 'how to check if image is copyrighted', 'what to do if someone stole your photo', 'free images for commercial use'. Target photographers and bloggers.
seo_pages: how to check if an image is copyrighted
free images for commercial use guide
execution_plan.technical_notes: Use browser-based EXIF metadata extraction. Integrate with TinEye/Google Vision API for reverse search. CC license detection from metadata.

source_signals: HN Show: tool to find companies using photos without paying (#157)
r/photography: TV network used photograph without asking (#255)
r/photography: copyright on vintage photo prints (#301)
Google Trends Rising: related image tool queries
